title: Adenocalymma cristicalyx
intro: Adenocalymma cristicalyx, also known as the 'Brazilian Angel's Trumpet', is a plant species in the family Bignoniaceae, native to the tropical regions of South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, fever, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Adenocalymma cristicalyx include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, particularly adenocalyxin A, which has been shown to exhibit significant anti-inflammatory and antioxidant activities. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
